paper.title: The origin of early-type runaway stars from open clusters

review.summary: This paper presents a new p-value-based chi-squared distance minimization method to trace back runaway and walkaway stars to their parent open clusters. The main data assets include: (1) statistical reference tables defining significance thresholds for the fitting procedure; (2) spatial extent measurements of clusters justifying the 1 pc encounter threshold; (3) kinematic results for ONC runaway candidates from literature samples; (4) updated radial velocities and space velocities for the classical Hoogerwerf runaway sample, reclassifying half as walkaways; (5) comprehensive parent-cluster identification tables for both disk and halo runaways, including flight times, ejection velocities, cluster ages, and suggested ejection mechanisms (BSS vs DES); (6) appendix tables compiling astrometric data from Hipparcos, Gaia DR2, and EDR3 for the runaway sample and for candidate clusters; (7) kinematic parameters for Galactic halo B-type runaways from Fuhrmeister (2021). Two local machine-readable CSV files (Hypervel_Hooglist.csv and cluster_parameters.csv) back the pgfplotstable-generated appendix tables. External resources include the ESA Gaia mission portal and DPAC, plus acknowledged use of SIMBAD, VizieR, and TOPCAT.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Statistical significance reference table defining p-value thresholds for 1-parameter and 8-parameter chi-squared distributions, used to set the 2-sigma rejection limit in the distance minimization procedure.

source_refs.caption: P-values corresponding to different significance levels for 1 and 8 parameters using the $\chi^2$ distribution when all parameters are at the respective ends of the uncertainty.
source_refs.label: tab:significance

columns.name: Significance $\alpha$
columns.meaning: Statistical significance level expressed in standard deviations

columns.name: P-val(1 Parameter)
columns.meaning: P-value for a single-parameter chi-squared distribution

columns.name: P-val(8 Parameters)
columns.meaning: P-value for an 8-parameter chi-squared distribution

internal_tables.evidence: P-values corresponding to different significance levels for 1 and 8 parameters using the $\chi^2$ distribution when all parameters are at the respective ends of the uncertainty.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Spatial extent of newly studied open clusters, providing the radius containing 50% of member stars to justify the 1 pc close-encounter threshold.

source_refs.caption: Radius containing 50\% of the member stars of new clusters reported in this paper.
source_refs.label: tab:extentofclusters

columns.name: Cluster
columns.meaning: Name of the open cluster

columns.name: Extent (pc)
columns.meaning: Radius containing 50% of the cluster member stars

internal_tables.evidence: Radius containing 50\% of the member stars of new clusters reported in this paper.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Minimization results for 26 ONC three-dimensional runaway candidates compiled from Schoettler+2020 and Farias+, showing p-values, flight times, and closest distances using Gaia DR2 and EDR3 astrometry.

source_refs.caption: P values for the three-dimensional runaway samples of \citetads{2020MNRAS.495.3104S} and \citetads{Farias_orion}, thought to come from the ONC.
source_refs.label: tab:schoetller

columns.name: Number
columns.meaning: Sequential identifier for the star in the combined sample

columns.name: Gaia DR2 Source ID
columns.meaning: Gaia DR2 unique source identifier

columns.name: P2
columns.meaning: P-value from the distance minimization using Gaia DR2 astrometry

columns.name: T$_f$ (DR2)
columns.meaning: Best-fit time of flight to the ONC using Gaia DR2

columns.name: P3
columns.meaning: P-value from the distance minimization using Gaia EDR3 astrometry

columns.name: T$_f$ (EDR3)
columns.meaning: Best-fit time of flight to the ONC using Gaia EDR3

columns.name: D$_{p,2}$
columns.meaning: Minimum distance between the star and ONC center using DR2

columns.name: D$_{p,3}$
columns.meaning: Minimum distance between the star and ONC center using EDR3

internal_tables.evidence: P values for the three-dimensional runaway samples of \citetads{2020MNRAS.495.3104S} and \citetads{Farias_orion}, thought to come from the ONC.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Updated radial velocities and derived space velocities for the Hoogerwerf et al. runaway sample, distinguishing walkaway stars (v_s < 30 km/s) from runaway stars.

source_refs.caption: Radial velocities v$_\textrm{rad}$ and derived space velocities v$_\textrm{s}$ for the sample of \citetads{hoog}.
source_refs.label: tab:spacevelocity

columns.name: HIP
columns.meaning: Hipparcos catalogue identifier

columns.name: RVV
columns.meaning: Radial velocity variability flag (Y = variable/binary, N = single)

columns.name: v$_\textrm{rad}$
columns.meaning: Heliocentric radial velocity (systemic velocity for spectroscopic binaries)

columns.name: v$_\textrm{s}$
columns.meaning: Space velocity with respect to Galactic rotation (Model I)

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Literature reference for the adopted radial velocity

internal_tables.evidence: Radial velocities v$_\textrm{rad}$ and derived space velocities v$_\textrm{s}$ for the sample of \citetads{hoog}.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Main result table summarizing suggested parent clusters for the Hoogerwerf sample runaways and walkaways, including p-values, flight times, ejection velocities, cluster ages, age-compatibility flags, and ejection mechanisms.

source_refs.caption: Suggested Clusters as places of origin for runaway stars based on spatial coincidence.
source_refs.label: tab:identification

columns.name: HIP Num
columns.meaning: Hipparcos catalogue identifier

columns.name: Origin
columns.meaning: Suggested parent open cluster or association

columns.name: p-val
columns.meaning: Statistical p-value for the best-fit trajectory from the minimization procedure

columns.name: T$_\textrm{f}$
columns.meaning: Time of flight to the parent cluster computed in the Galactic potential

columns.name: T$_\textrm{rel}$
columns.meaning: Relative time of flight assuming a straight-line trajectory

columns.name: V$_\textrm{rel}$
columns.meaning: Relative ejection velocity of the star with respect to the parent cluster

columns.name: T$_\textrm{c}$
columns.meaning: Published age of the parent cluster

columns.name: Flag$_\textrm{age}$
columns.meaning: Flag indicating whether the cluster age is compatible with the suggested origin mechanism (Y = compatible, N = incompatible)

columns.name: Suggested Mechanism
columns.meaning: Ejection mechanism suggested by this study (BSS = binary supernova scenario, DES = dynamical ejection scenario)

columns.name: Original Mechanism
columns.meaning: Ejection mechanism originally suggested by Hoogerwerf et al.

columns.name: References
columns.meaning: Literature references for the kinematic and age parameters

internal_tables.evidence: Suggested Clusters as places of origin for runaway stars based on spatial coincidence.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Results for Galactic halo B-type runaway stars, listing candidate parent clusters, p-values, flight times, ejection velocities, cluster ages, and suggested ejection mechanisms.

source_refs.caption: Possible Clusters as sites of origin of the Halo runaway stars using spatial coincidence.
source_refs.label: tab:haloidenfication

columns.name: Star
columns.meaning: Name or identifier of the halo runaway star

columns.name: Cluster Name
columns.meaning: Candidate parent open cluster

columns.name: p-val
columns.meaning: P-value for the best-fit trajectory using EDR3 proper motions

columns.name: T$_f$
columns.meaning: Time of flight computed in the Galactic potential

columns.name: T$_{rel}$
columns.meaning: Relative time of flight assuming a straight-line trajectory

columns.name: v$_\textrm{rel}$
columns.meaning: Relative ejection velocity with respect to the parent cluster

columns.name: T$_c$
columns.meaning: Age of the candidate parent cluster

columns.name: Flag$_{age}$
columns.meaning: Flag indicating temporal compatibility of cluster age with star origin (Y = possible, N = ruled out)

columns.name: Suggested mechanism
columns.meaning: Suggested ejection mechanism (BSS or DES)

columns.name: References
columns.meaning: Literature references for cluster kinematics and ages

columns.name: col_011
columns.meaning: Unspecified extra column (empty in the source table)

internal_tables.evidence: Possible Clusters as sites of origin of the Halo runaway stars using spatial coincidence.

external_resources.description: ESA Gaia mission data portal, the primary source of astrometric (proper motions, parallaxes) and photometric data used in this study.

source_refs.context: This work has made use of data from the European Space Agency (ESA) mission {\it Gaia} (\url{https://www.cosmos.esa.int/gaia}), processed by the {\it Gaia} Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC, \url{https://www.cosmos.esa.int/web/gaia/dpac/consortium}).

external_resources.evidence: Acknowledged in the paper as the source of Gaia DR2 and EDR3 astrometric data used for the kinematic traceback of runaway stars.

external_resources.description: Gaia Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC) website, responsible for processing and calibrating Gaia survey data.

external_resources.evidence: Acknowledged in the paper as the processing pipeline for the Gaia data releases used in this work.

external_resources.description: SIMBAD astronomical database, VizieR catalog access tool, and TOPCAT table processing software, used for cross-matching and visualizing astronomical catalogues.

source_refs.context: This research has made use of the SIMBAD database, operated at CDS, the VizieR catalog access tool, CDS, Strasbourg, France (DOI : 10.26093/cds/vizier), and TOPCAT table processing software.
